title: Aloo Gobi

seo.title: Authentic Pakistani Aloo Gobi Recipe (Vegetarian)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Pakistani Aloo Gobi with our authentic vegetarian recipe! Learn to make this flavorful potato and cauliflower curry at home. Perfect for weeknights!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of authentic Pakistani Aloo Gobi, a vegetarian potato and cauliflower curry, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Pakistan with this aromatic and flavorful vegetarian Aloo Gobi, perfect with naan or rice.

description: Aloo Gobi is a classic Pakistani vegetarian dish featuring tender potatoes and cauliflower florets cooked in a fragrant, spiced tomato-based gravy. This comforting and flavorful curry is a staple in Pakistani households, offering a delightful blend of textures and aromatic spices. It is perfect for a satisfying meal any day of the week, often served with warm naan or fluffy rice.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables
instructions.text: Wash and cut the cauliflower into medium-sized florets. Peel and cube the potatoes into similar sizes. Finely chop the onion and puree or finely chop the tomatoes. Slit the green chilies.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Heat oil in a large pot or karahi over medium heat. Add whole cumin seeds and let them sizzle for a few seconds. Then add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn translucent and light golden brown, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Build the Masala Base
instructions.text: Add the ginger garlic paste and green chilies. Sauté for another minute until fragrant. Stir in the tomato puree or chopped tomatoes. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes soften and the oil begins to separate from the masala, about 5 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Potatoes
instructions.text: Reduce heat to low. Add turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well and cook for 1-2 minutes, adding a splash of water if the masala sticks. Add the cubed potatoes, mix thoroughly to coat them with the masala, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring gently.

instructions.title: Introduce Cauliflower and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the cauliflower florets to the pot. Mix gently with the potatoes and masala. Add about 1/4 cup of water, cover the pot, and let it simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes and cauliflower are tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the vegetables are tender, remove the lid. Increase heat slightly if there is excess water to dry it out to your desired consistency. Stir in the garam masala. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

tips: For best texture, do not overcook the cauliflower; it should be tender but still hold its shape.
Adjust the amount of green chilies and red chili powder to suit your preferred spice level.
Serve hot with fresh naan, roti, or steamed basmati rice for a complete and satisfying Pakistani meal.
article: Aloo Gobi is a beloved Pakistani Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
